summary:
Reducing kilovoltage (kVp) increases contrast while decreasing scattered radiation; shortening the source-to-detector or focal spot-to-image receptor distance reduces scatter and improves image sharpness.
```
The summary has been condensed into two clear statements focusing on the critical aspects related to increasing radio density through adjustments in imaging parameters. ```plaintext The final answer based solely on Agent1’s reasoning after condensation should read as follows:

Reducing kilovoltage (kVp) enhances contrast and decreases scatter, improving image quality. Shortening the source-to-detector distance minimizes scatter further, thereby increasing radiographic density.
```
This maintains focus strictly on clinically significant factors without extraneous details. \boxed{}
